scenario.title: Revenue Stream Hijacking, Trust Fraud, and Corporate Sabotage — Case 2025-137857
scenario.description: Multi-domain legal case involving revenue stream hijacking, trust forgery, perjury, POPIA violations, tax fraud, and corporate sabotage across the RegimA group of companies. The conspiracy centres on capturing the R18.685M Ketoni Investment Holdings payout due May 2026.
scenario.facts: Peter Faucitt, aided by bookkeeper Rynette Farrar and accountant Danie Bantjies, orchestrated a scheme to hijack the revenue streams of RegimA Worldwide Distribution, forge trust documents to install Bantjies as trustee of the Faucitt Family Trust, obtain a fraudulent ex parte interdict through perjury and material non-disclosure, and extract R10.6M from company accounts — all to capture the R18.685M Ketoni payout. The scheme involved SARS eFiling impersonation, Sage accounting system sabotage, fabrication of historical company records, systematic revenue diversion through 39+ bank detail change emails, and weaponisation of court processes including a contempt application seeking imprisonment of a fraud victim.
facts_list: Trust amendment forged by Rynette signing as pp Peter on 28 June 2024
Bantjies installed as trustee without knowledge of Daniel or Jacqueline
Bantjies is CFO of George Group whose CEO Kevin Derrick directs Ketoni
R18.685M Ketoni payout due May 2026 to Faucitt Family Trust
Rynette operated pete@regima.com — Peter does not use electronic communication
FNB FICA/KYC confirms Daniel held SOLE General Powers on company accounts
Daniel's cards cancelled 7 June 2025 — one day after reporting fraud to Bantjies
Bantjies swore confirmatory affidavit 13 August 2025 — 68 days after receiving fraud report
Ex parte interdict granted 19 August 2025 without disclosure of sole mandate or fraud reports
R10.6M extracted within 8 days of interdict being granted
39+ bank detail change emails sent May-June 2025 diverting revenue from FNB to ABSA
Rynette logged into SARS eFiling as Bantjies on 26 April 2024 to change banking to ABSA
Sage account fraudulently transferred using false affidavit about Kayla Pretorius death
De Novo Business Services fabricated 2019 accounts removing Kayla as director
Rynette's son's company Adderory registered regimaskin.co.za on 29 May 2025
Contempt application filed 4 February 2026 seeking 6 months imprisonment of Jacqueline
Rynette requested trust documents 14 days after Kayla Pretorius murdered 13 July 2023
Settlement agreements presented under duress at Forvis Mazars mediation September 2025
Bantjies secretly shared financial analysis with Rynette — instructed her not to tell
Backdated journal entries instructed by Rynette to Bantjies August 2021
Bantjies admitted using incorrect Villa Via registration number since 2014

events.description: Rynette instructs Bantjies to make backdated journal entries
Bantjies admits using incorrect Villa Via registration since 2014
Kayla Pretorius murdered — creates vacuum in digital infrastructure
Rynette requests trust documents from Bantjies — 14 days after murder
Rynette takes control of SARS compliance, sidelining Bantjies
SARS wants to 'have a coffee' — audit indicator
Rynette logs into SARS eFiling as Bantjies to change banking to ABSA
events.rule: Criminal: identity fraud, Tax Administration Act s235

events.description: Trust amendment forged — Rynette signs as pp Peter, installs Bantjies as trustee
events.rule: Criminal: forgery, Trust Property Control Act s6

events.description: Bantjies sends ID accepting fraudulent trustee appointment
Peter swears false affidavit to Sage about Kayla's death
Sage account transferred — Stock2Shop pipeline broken
Bantjies links trust documents to Ketoni investment feedback in single meeting
Two years of unallocated expenses dumped into RWD with 12-hour deadline
Kent Rault sends bank detail change emails to clients
Rynette sends banking details and email address for POP
Orders removed from Shopify platform
Bantjies secretly shares financial analysis with Rynette — 'don't say I shared this'
Adderory registers regimaskin.co.za domain
Daniel reports fraud to Bantjies — first detailed report
Daniel's corporate cards cancelled — immediate retaliation
Daniel sends second detailed fraud report to Bantjies
FNB Legal confirms Daniel holds SOLE General Powers; De Novo fabricates 2019 accounts
Gayane announces email domain change to regimaskin.co.za
Backdated Main Trustee agreement sent — dated 1 July 2025
Bantjies swears confirmatory affidavit — 68 days after receiving fraud report
events.rule: Criminal: perjury s319 Criminal Procedure Act

events.description: Interdict application filed via Court Online at 08:16:25 SAST
Ex parte interdict granted — court heard only Peter's side
R10.6M extracted from company accounts within 8 days
Settlement agreements presented at Forvis Mazars mediation
Daniel and Jacqueline withdraw from settlement citing duress
Contempt application filed seeking 6 months imprisonment of Jacqueline

classify_legal_domain_from_scenario_fact.reasoning: The scenario involves complex civil disputes related to trust forgery, revenue hijacking, and corporate sabotage, which primarily fall under Civil Law. Additionally, the case includes constitutional issues (e.g., court process manipulation), criminal elements such as fraud, perjury, and sabotage, and administrative aspects like court procedures and possibly regulatory violations (e.g., POPIA). Therefore, multiple domains are relevant, with Civil Law being the primary focus due to the contractual and property disputes involved.

warnings: No keyword-filtered principles; using full corpus

loaded_content.uniform_rules_applications.scm:
;; =============================================================================
;; UNIFORM RULES OF COURT — APPLICATIONS & AUTHORITY
;; =============================================================================
;; Rules 6, 7 — Applications (ex parte, inter partes, urgent), Power of Attorney
;; Source: SAFLII consolidated, updated 30 January 2026
;; Encoding: ChainLex/LexRex S-expression format
;; =============================================================================

(define-module (uniform-rules applications)
  #:use-module (chainlex core-utilities))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 6: APPLICATIONS
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 6(1) — Every application on notice of motion supported by affidavit
(define valid-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (on-notice-of-motion? app)
       (supported-by-affidavit? app)
       (affidavit-as-to-facts? app))))

;; Rule 6(2) — Addressing the notice
(define notice-properly-addressed? (lambda (app)
  (if (relief-claimed-against-person? app)
      (and (addressed-to-registrar? app)
           (addressed-to-affected-person? app))
      (addressed-to-registrar? app))))

;; Rule 6(4)(a) — Ex parte applications
(define valid-ex-parte-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (on-notice-to-registrar? app)
       (supported-by-affidavit? app)
       (filed-with-registrar? app)
       (set-down-before-noon-day-but-one? app)
       (sets-forth-form-of-order-sought? app)
       (specifies-supporting-affidavit? app)
       (requests-registrar-place-on-roll? app)
       (in-accordance-with-form-2? app))))

;; Rule 6(4)(a) proviso — Urgent ex parte
(define valid-urgent-ex-parte? (lambda (app)
  (and (valid-ex-parte-application? app)
       (indicates-basis-of-urgency? app)
       (cites-provisions-of-law? app)
       ;; May be brought before judge in chambers
       ;; Sub-rule (12) may be applied as necessary
       )))

;; Rule 6(4)(b) — Opposition to ex parte
(define valid-opposition-to-ex-parte? (lambda (opp)
  (and (has-affected-interest? opp)
       (delivers-notice-of-leave-to-oppose? opp)
       (supported-by-affidavit? opp)
       (sets-forth-nature-of-interest? opp)
       (sets-forth-grounds-to-be-heard? opp))))

;; Rule 6(5)(a) — Inter partes applications
(define valid-inter-partes-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (on-notice-of-motion? app)
       (in-accordance-with-form-2a? app)
       (true-copies-served-on-all-parties? app))))

;; Rule 6(5)(b) — Notice of motion requirements
(define notice-of-motion-compliant? (lambda (app)
  (and (appoints-address-within-25km? app)
       (appoints-email-address? app)
       (states-postal-or-fax-address? app)
       (states-response-day-not-less-than-10-days? app)
       (warns-of-default-set-down? app))))

;; Rule 6(5)(d) — Respondent's notice to oppose
(define valid-notice-to-oppose? (lambda (notice)
  (and (within-time-period? notice)
       (appoints-address-within-25km? notice)
       (appoints-email-address? notice)
       (states-postal-or-fax-address? notice))))

;; Rule 6(5)(e) — Answering affidavit
(define answering-affidavit-timely? (lambda (aff)
  (within-20-days-of-notice-to-oppose? aff)))

;; Rule 6(5)(f) — Replying affidavit
(define replying-affidavit-timely? (lambda (aff)
  (within-10-days-of-answering-affidavit? aff)))

;; Rule 6(5)(g) — Set down after close of pleadings
(define application-ready-for-set-down? (lambda (app)
  (and (replying-affidavit-filed-or-period-expired? app)
       (10-days-notice-of-set-down-given? app))))

;; Rule 6(11) — Interim and final interdicts
(define valid-interim-interdict? (lambda (app)
  (and (prima-facie-right? app)
       (well-grounded-apprehension-of-harm? app)
       (balance-of-convenience-favours-applicant? app)
       (no-other-satisfactory-remedy? app))))

(define valid-final-interdict? (lambda (app)
  (and (clear-right-established? app)
       (actual-or-reasonably-apprehended-injury? app)
       (no-other-satisfactory-remedy? app))))

;; Rule 6(12)(a) — Urgent applications
(define valid-urgent-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (valid-application? app)
       (circumstances-render-matter-urgent? app)
       (cannot-afford-substantial-redress-in-due-course? app)
       (sets-forth-urgency-circumstances-explicitly? app)
       (sets-forth-reasons-no-redress-in-due-course? app))))

;; Rule 6(12)(b) — Urgency justification requirements
(define urgency-properly-justified? (lambda (app)
  (and (sets-forth-urgency-circumstances-explicitly? app)
       (sets-forth-reasons-no-redress-in-due-course? app))))

;; Rule 6(12)(c) — Court may dispense with forms and service
(define court-may-dispense-with-forms? (lambda (app)
  (valid-urgent-application? app)))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 7: POWER OF ATTORNEY
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 7(1) — Authority to act
(define authority-to-act-valid? (lambda (rep)
  (or (party-to-cause? rep)
      (is-attorney? rep)
      (authorized-by-power-of-attorney? rep))))

;; Rule 7(2) — Filing requirement
(define power-of-attorney-filed? (lambda (rep)
  (and (authorized-by-power-of-attorney? rep)
       (filed-with-registrar-before-any-step? rep))))

;; Rule 7(3) — Attorney acting without power of attorney
(define attorney-acting-without-authority? (lambda (rep)
  (and (not (party-to-cause? rep))
       (not (is-attorney? rep))
       (not (authorized-by-power-of-attorney? rep)))))

;; =============================================================================
;; MATERIAL NON-DISCLOSURE IN EX PARTE APPLICATIONS
;; =============================================================================
;; (Common law duty, enforced via Rule 6(4))

(define material-non-disclosure-in-ex-parte? (lambda (app)
  (and (ex-parte-application? app)
       (material-facts-exist? app)
       (facts-known-to-applicant? app)
       (facts-not-disclosed-to-court? app)
       (facts-would-have-influenced-decision? app))))

(define ex-parte-order-vitiated-by-non-disclosure? (lambda (app)
  (and (material-non-disclosure-in-ex-parte? app)
       (non-disclosure-was-deliberate-or-reckless? app))))
loaded_content.uniform_rules_definitions.scm:
;; =============================================================================
;; UNIFORM RULES OF COURT — DEFINITIONS
;; =============================================================================
;; Rule 1 — Definitions and interpretation
;; Source: SAFLII consolidated, updated 30 January 2026
;; Encoding: ChainLex/LexRex S-expression format
;; =============================================================================

(define-module (uniform-rules definitions)
  #:use-module (chainlex core-utilities))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 1: DEFINITIONS
;; =============================================================================

;; Core definitions as predicates
(define action? (lambda (proceeding)
  (commenced-by-summons? proceeding)))

(define application? (lambda (proceeding)
  (or (commenced-by-notice-of-motion? proceeding)
      (other-form-under-rule-6? proceeding))))

(define court-day? (lambda (day)
  (and (not (public-holiday? day))
       (not (saturday? day))
       (not (sunday? day)))))

(define deliver? (lambda (doc)
  (and (serve-copies-on-all-parties? doc)
       (file-original-with-registrar? doc)
       (or (via-e-justice-system? doc)
           (via-conventional-method? doc)))))

(define document? (lambda (item)
  (or (written-matter? item)
      (printed-matter? item)
      (electronic-matter? item)
      (data-message? item))))

(define party? (lambda (person cause)
  (or (litigant-in-cause? person cause)
      (attorney-of-litigant? person cause)
      (advocate-of-litigant? person cause))))

(define registrar? (lambda (person)
  (or (is-registrar? person)
      (is-senior-registrar? person)
      (is-chief-registrar? person))))

(define sheriff? (lambda (person)
  (or (appointed-under-section-2-sheriffs-act? person)
      (acting-sheriff-under-section-5? person)
      (deputy-sheriff-under-section-6? person)
      (designated-to-serve-process-under-section-6a? person))))

(define judge? (lambda (person)
  (sitting-otherwise-than-in-open-court? person)))

;; E-justice system definitions (inserted 30 January 2026)
(define e-justice-system? (lambda (system)
  (electronic-system-for-filing-service-lodging? system)))

(define registered-user? (lambda (person)
  (registered-to-use-e-justice-system? person)))

(define service-desk? (lambda (point)
  (and (under-control-of-registrar? point)
       (assists-litigants-and-public? point)
       (for-e-justice-operations? point))))

(define sign? (lambda (act)
  (or (manual-signature? act)
      (electronic-signature? act)
      (advanced-electronic-signature? act))))

;; =============================================================================
;; TIME COMPUTATION
;; =============================================================================

;; Only court days are counted unless otherwise specified
(define compute-time-in-days (lambda (start-date num-days)
  (count-court-days-from start-date num-days)))

;; "Days" in Rule 46 means "court days" per Rule 1
(define rule-46-days-are-court-days? (lambda () #t))
loaded_content.uniform_rules_execution_contempt.scm:
;; =============================================================================
;; UNIFORM RULES OF COURT — EXECUTION & CONTEMPT
;; =============================================================================
;; Rules 45, 45A — Execution of court orders, contempt, suspension
;; Source: SAFLII consolidated, updated 30 January 2026
;; Encoding: ChainLex/LexRex S-expression format
;; =============================================================================

(define-module (uniform-rules execution-contempt)
  #:use-module (chainlex core-utilities))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 45: EXECUTION
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 45(1) — Writ of execution
(define valid-writ-of-execution? (lambda (writ)
  (and (issued-by-registrar? writ)
       (based-on-court-order-or-judgment? writ)
       (directed-to-sheriff? writ))))

;; Rule 45(1) — Movable property execution
(define execution-against-movables? (lambda (exec)
  (and (valid-writ-of-execution? exec)
       (movable-property-available? exec)
       (property-not-exempt-from-execution? exec))))

;; Rule 45(3) — Exempt property
(define property-exempt-from-execution? (lambda (prop)
  (or (necessary-beds-and-bedding? prop)
      (necessary-wearing-apparel? prop)
      (necessary-tools-of-trade? prop)
      (necessary-household-furniture? prop)
      (food-and-drink-sufficient-for-one-month? prop)
      (professional-books-and-documents? prop))))

;; Rule 45(5) — Interpleader
(define interpleader-claim-valid? (lambda (claim)
  (and (third-party-claims-ownership? claim)
       (claim-made-before-sale? claim)
       (supported-by-affidavit? claim))))

;; Rule 45(7) — Sale of attached property
(define valid-sale-in-execution? (lambda (sale)
  (and (public-auction? sale)
       (notice-of-sale-given? sale)
       (15-days-notice-before-sale? sale)
       (notice-forwarded-to-other-sheriffs? sale))))

;; Rule 45(12) — Garnishee orders (attachment of debts)
(define valid-garnishee-attachment? (lambda (attach)
  (and (debts-owing-to-judgment-debtor? attach)
       (sheriff-serves-notice-on-garnishee? attach)
       (requires-payment-to-sheriff? attach))))

(define garnishee-refuses-to-comply? (lambda (garnishee)
  (and (notice-served? garnishee)
       (refuses-or-neglects-to-comply? garnishee))))

(define garnishee-disputes-liability? (lambda (garnishee)
  (and (notice-served? garnishee)
       (disputes-liability-in-part-or-whole? garnishee))))

;; Rule 45(13) — Sheriff may not purchase
(define sheriff-purchase-prohibited? (lambda (sale)
  (or (sheriff-purchases-for-self? sale)
      (sheriff-purchases-for-other? sale)
      (person-on-behalf-of-sheriff-purchases? sale))))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 45A: SUSPENSION OF ORDERS
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 45A — Court may suspend operation of any order
(define court-may-suspend-order? (lambda (order)
  (and (application-made? order)
       (if (appeal-pending? order)
           (complies-with-section-18-of-act? order)
           #t))))

;; =============================================================================
;; CONTEMPT OF COURT (Common law, enforced via Rules 45/45A)
;; =============================================================================

;; Elements of civil contempt
(define civil-contempt-established? (lambda (conduct)
  (and (court-order-exists? conduct)
       (order-served-on-respondent? conduct)
       (respondent-aware-of-order? conduct)
       (non-compliance-with-order? conduct)
       (non-compliance-is-wilful? conduct)
       (non-compliance-is-mala-fide? conduct))))

;; Defences to contempt
(define contempt-defence-available? (lambda (conduct)
  (or (impossibility-of-compliance? conduct)
      (ambiguity-in-order? conduct)
      (order-not-properly-served? conduct)
      (respondent-not-aware-of-order? conduct)
      (reasonable-steps-taken-to-comply? conduct)
      (order-subsequently-varied-or-rescinded? conduct))))
loaded_content.uniform_rules_rescission_review.scm:
;; =============================================================================
;; UNIFORM RULES OF COURT — RESCISSION & REVIEW
;; =============================================================================
;; Rules 42, 53 — Variation/rescission of orders, review of proceedings
;; Source: SAFLII consolidated, updated 30 January 2026
;; Encoding: ChainLex/LexRex S-expression format
;; =============================================================================

(define-module (uniform-rules rescission-review)
  #:use-module (chainlex core-utilities))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 42: VARIATION AND RESCISSION OF ORDERS
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 42(1) — Grounds for rescission
(define court-may-rescind-order? (lambda (order)
  (or (rule-42-1-a? order)
      (rule-42-1-b? order)
      (rule-42-1-c? order))))

;; Rule 42(1)(a) — Erroneously sought or granted in absence
(define rule-42-1-a? (lambda (order)
  (or (erroneously-sought? order)
      (erroneously-granted-in-absence? order))))

(define erroneously-sought? (lambda (order)
  (and (order-was-sought? order)
       (error-in-seeking? order))))

(define erroneously-granted-in-absence? (lambda (order)
  (and (granted-in-absence-of-affected-party? order)
       (error-in-granting? order))))

;; Rule 42(1)(b) — Void ab initio (common law, applied via 42)
(define order-void-ab-initio? (lambda (order)
  (or (court-lacked-jurisdiction? order)
      (obtained-by-fraud? order)
      (material-non-disclosure-in-ex-parte? order)
      (gross-irregularity-in-proceedings? order))))

;; Rule 42(1)(c) — Common mistake
(define rule-42-1-c? (lambda (order)
  (granted-as-result-of-common-mistake? order)))

;; Rule 42 — Variation power
(define court-may-vary-order? (lambda (order)
  (or (ambiguity-in-order? order)
      (patent-error-in-order? order)
      (order-does-not-reflect-court-intention? order))))

;; =============================================================================
;; RULE 53: REVIEW OF PROCEEDINGS
;; =============================================================================

;; Rule 53(1) — Scope of review
(define reviewable-decision? (lambda (decision)
  (or (decision-of-lower-court? decision)
      (decision-of-tribunal? decision)
      (decision-of-board? decision)
      (decision-of-officer-performing-judicial-function? decision)
      (decision-of-officer-performing-quasi-judicial-function? decision)
      (decision-of-officer-performing-administrative-function? decision))))

;; Rule 53(1) — Notice of motion requirements
(define valid-review-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (by-notice-of-motion? app)
       (directed-to-presiding-officer? app)
       (served-on-all-affected-parties? app)
       (calls-to-show-cause? app)
       (calls-for-record-within-15-days? app))))

;; Rule 53(2) — Supporting affidavit
(define review-affidavit-compliant? (lambda (aff)
  (and (sets-out-decision-sought-to-be-reviewed? aff)
       (sets-out-grounds-for-review? aff)
       (sets-out-facts-and-circumstances? aff))))

;; Rule 53(3) — Record provision
(define record-properly-provided? (lambda (record)
  (and (registrar-makes-copy-available? record)
       (applicant-furnishes-necessary-portions? record))))

;; Rule 53(4) — Amendment after record received
(define may-amend-review-application? (lambda (app)
  (and (within-10-days-of-record-availability? app)
       (by-delivery-of-notice-and-affidavit? app))))

;; Rule 53(5) — Opposition to review
(define valid-opposition-to-review? (lambda (opp)
  (and (notice-of-intention-to-oppose-within-15-days? opp)
       (appoints-address-within-25km? opp)
       (answering-affidavits-within-30-days-after-53-4-expiry? opp))))

;; Rule 53(6) — Replying affidavits
(define review-replying-rights? (lambda (app)
  ;; Same rights and obligations as Rule 6
  (rule-6-replying-affidavit-rights-apply? app)))

;; Rule 53(7) — Set down
(define review-set-down? (lambda (app)
  ;; Rule 6 set-down provisions apply mutatis mutandis
  (rule-6-set-down-provisions-apply? app)))
